name: MaintainX Work Order & CMMS API
what_it_does: MaintainX Work Order and CMMS REST API for mobile-first maintenance management and operations. Enables AI agents to manage digital work order creation and assignment for field maintenance automation, handle preventive maintenance scheduling and PM triggers for reliability operations, access asset and equipment tracking with maintenance history for lifecycle management, retrieve parts inventory and parts consumption tracking for maintenance procurement, manage team communication and procedure documentation for operations knowledge management, handle meter and reading integration for usage-based maintenance triggers, access operational analytics and MTTR/MTBF reporting for reliability KPIs, retrieve audit and inspection management for compliance operations, manage contractor and vendor work order management for outsourced maintenance, and integrate MaintainX with ERP, IoT, and operations management platforms.
use_cases: Creating work orders from operations agents
Triggering PM schedules from reliability agents
Tracking parts consumption from maintenance procurement agents
Managing inspections from compliance operations agents
not_for: Large enterprise requiring full EAM with financial integration
FDA-regulated equipment without GAMP 5 validated CMMS
Construction project management beyond ongoing maintenance
best_when: A manufacturer, facility, or operations team using MaintainX wants AI agents to automate work order management, preventive maintenance, asset tracking, parts procurement, inspection management, and reliability analytics.
avoid_when: REGULATED EQUIPMENT MAINTENANCE: Automated maintenance management for FDA-regulated medical devices (21 CFR Part 820) or pharmaceutical manufacturing equipment (21 CFR Part 211) requires GAMP 5 validated CMMS; automated MaintainX integration for regulated equipment without validation creates FDA inspection finding risk. OSHA safety-critical maintenance — automated work orders for equipment requiring OSHA lockout/tagout, confined space entry, or hot work permit must integrate safety permit workflows; automated work orders bypassing safety permitting create OSHA violation risk. Inspection regulatory compliance — automated inspection management for boilers, pressure vessels, fire protection, and elevators must comply with state-specific inspection laws; automated completion without required inspector certification creates safety code violation.

auth.notes: MaintainX uses API key and OAuth 2.0 with scopes. REST API with JSON. Documentation at developer.getmaintainx.com. San Francisco, California HQ. Founded 2018. Private (Bessemer Venture Partners, Index Ventures, $195M Series C). Mobile-first CMMS with 8,000+ customers. Competes with Limble CMMS, UpKeep, and Fiix for cloud maintenance management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan for small teams; limited users and features
pricing.paid_tiers: Monthly subscription; per-user pricing with feature tiers
pricing.notes: San Francisco. Founded 2018. Bessemer/Index-backed $195M. 8,000+ customers. Mobile-first CMMS. Competes with Limble and UpKeep.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; documented in developer portal

known_agent_gotchas: REGULATED EQUIPMENT CMMS VALIDATION: Automated maintenance management for FDA-regulated equipment requires GAMP 5 validated CMMS; MaintainX is not pre-validated for GxP; automated integration for pharmaceutical or medical device equipment requires formal validation

OSHA safety permit integration — automated work orders for equipment requiring OSHA safety permits (lockout/tagout, hot work, confined space) must integrate safety permit authorization workflows; automated work orders bypassing safety permitting create life safety OSHA violation risk

Preventive maintenance calendar accuracy — automated PM scheduling must account for asset calendar (production schedules, shutdown windows); PM triggers during production create operational disruption; implement production calendar awareness in PM scheduling

Parts availability before PM scheduling — automated PM work order creation should check parts availability for scheduled maintenance requiring specific parts; scheduling PM without confirmed parts availability creates maintenance delays

Webhook reliability for operations workflows — verify MaintainX webhook delivery reliability for operations-critical events (work order creation, completion); operations automation dependent on webhook delivery requires fallback polling

Contractor work order visibility — automated contractor work order management must implement appropriate data access controls; contractors should not have visibility to other customers' maintenance data through API integrations
agent_readiness.security_notes: Maintenance management. SOC2. API key/OAuth2 with scopes. US/EU. Equipment and operations data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Maintenance management. SOC2. OAuth2 with scopes. US/EU. Equipment and operations data.
